zh.lifeSummary: Matilda de Carvile是Adam De Careville的女兒，於1150年出生在英國維斯特莫藍德格亞特 馬斯格雷夫。她於1166年與Lord Robert de Musgrave在英國維斯特莫藍德結婚。他們至少是7個兒子的父母。她去世於1207年之後在英國。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Matilda de Carvile是Adam De Careville的女儿，于1150年出生在英国維斯特莫藍德格亞特 馬斯格雷夫。她于1166年与Lord Robert de Musgrave在英国維斯特莫藍德结婚。他们至少是7个儿子的父母。她去世于1207年之后在英国。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Carvile
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Concetto, Saverio.
Italian: probably a variant of   Cardillo  .

variants.name: Cardillo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rocco, Antonio, Carmine, Pasquale, Luigi, Salvatore, Oreste, Pasco, Raffaele, Serafino, Alfio.
Italian (Sicily): from cardillu ‘goldfinch’, possibly applied as a nickname for someone with a good singing voice or who wore colorful clothing.

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

en.lifeSummary: Matilda de Carvile was born in 1150, in Great Musgrave, Westmorland, England as the daughter of Adam De Careville. She married Lord Robert de Musgrave in 1166, in Westmorland, England. They were the parents of at least 7 sons. She died after 1207, in England.

ja.lifeSummary: Matilda de Carvileは1150年にイギリスウエストモーランドグレアト ムスグラブでAdam De Carevilleの娘として生まれた。1166年にイギリスウエストモーランドでLord Robert de Musgraveと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子の親であった。彼女は1207年以後にイギリスで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Matilda de Carvile(은)는 1150년 영국 웨스트모를랜드 그리트 무스그레이브에서 Adam De Careville의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1166년 영국 웨스트모를랜드에서 Lord Robert de Musgrave(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1207년 이후 영국에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Matilda de Carvile родилась в 1150 г. в местности грит мусграв, вестморланд, Англия. Её отца звали Adam De Careville. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Lord Robert de Musgrave в 1166 г. в местности вестморланд, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей. Она умерла после 1207 г. в местности Англия.
